steps.title: Use the missing-evidence clue
steps.content: The passage says much evidence of Hatshepsut's reign was deliberately destroyed. Because important evidence is gone, the account we are unlikely to have would be complete and thorough.

steps.title: Reject unrelated qualities
steps.content: A concerns creativity, not completeness. B means shallow, but missing evidence does not make a shallow account impossible. D concerns reliability, while the passage focuses on the lack of enough evidence. The answer is C.
